original: Poecilotheria rajaei, a giant tarantula with a 20cm leg span, is discovered in Sri Lanka
Hans Riegel Sr., German confectioner and inventor of  the 'gummy bear' (founder of Haribo), born in Bonn, Germany (d. 1945)
Rare 1938 copy of the comic (Action Comics No. 1) that first introduced Superman sells for a record US$6 million at action
Microsoft is founded as a partnership betweenBill Gatesand Paul Allen to develop and sell BASIC interpreters for the Altair 8800
Eddie Hill becomes the world's first driver to cover the quarter mile in under 5 seconds
Raoul Pictet, Swiss physicist who invented liquid nitrogen and was a pioneer of cryogenics, born in Geneva, Switzerland (d. 1929)
1st electric power plant fueled by garbage begins operating
Hank Aaronties Babe Ruth's home run record by hitting his 714th in Cincinnati against Jack Billingham
Wayne Gretzkysets NHL record with 213th point of season

Washington Capitals left wingAlex Ovechkinscores two goals in a 5-3 win over visiting Chicago Blackhawks to tie Wayne Gretzky at 894 for the most career goals in NHL history, and passes Jaromir Jagr for most game-winners with his 136th
Haskell Karp receives the 1st temporary artificial heart, implanted by surgeon Denton Cooley at Texas Heart Institute in Houston
Francis Drakeis knighted byQueen Elizabeth Iaboard his galleon, the "Golden Hind" at Deptford, England
Announcement of the discovery of gold in the Yukon

WWII: US Army Air Force bomber 'Lady Be Good' fails to return to
 Soluch, Libya base after its maiden flight; presumed lost at sea, wreck was discovered in desert 15 years later
Charles Darwinaboard the HMS Beagle reaches Rio de Janeiro
Susanna Madora Salter elected 1st US woman mayor in Argonia, Kansas
Casparus van Wooden patents chocolate milk powder in Amsterdam
Charles G. King, University of Pittsburgh isolates Vitamin C, using adrenal samples provided by Hungarian Albert Szent-Györgyi
Largest group of sunspots on record
The CND Peace Symbol displayed in public for the first time in London
Africa's tallest monument, the African Renaissance Monument, dedicated outside Dakar, Senegal, designed by Pierre Goudiaby to commemorate Senegal's 50th anniversary of independence
Apollo 6 launched atop Saturn V; unmanned
6th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Challenger 1 launches

Hugh Masekela, South African trumpeter, described as the "father of South African jazz" ("Grazing in the Grass"; "Soweto Blues"; Sarafina!), and anti-apartheid activist, born in Witbank, South Africa (d. 2018)
Estelle Harris, American actress and comedian (Estelle Costanza - Seinfeld; Toy Story), born in Manhattan, New York (d. 2022)
MLB Chicago Cubs Karl "Tuffy" Rhodes, hit 3 HRs in 1993 hits 3 HRs on Opening Day vs Mets starter Dwight "Doc" Gooden at Wrigley Field, Chicago
MLB New York Mets set Opening Day record with 6 HRs, in a 10-6 win over Expos at Montreal
Game 1 of Mayor Challenge - NY Yankees beat NY Mets 6-4 at Yank Stad
the Beatles' "Can't Buy Me Love" single goes #1 and stays #1 for 5 weeks
The first model of the new Saab Viggen fighter aircraft plane is unveiled.
Alibababecomes the world's largest retailer according to US Securities and Exchange Commission
Vice PresidentJohn Tylerbecomes the 10th President of the United States after the death of President William Henry Harrison
Earthquake in Kangra India, kills 20,000
Film serial "The Perils of Pauline" is shown for the first time in Los Angeles, California
South Africa all out 35 vs England (Trott 4-19, Haigh 6-11)
Space shuttle mission STS 83 (Columbia) is launched
SingerGloria Estefanreleased from hospital after her tour bus accident
Elmer Bernstein, American film music composer (The Magnificent Seven; To Kill A Mockingbird; The Age Of Innocence; Thoroughly Modern Millie), born in New York City (d. 2004)
David E. Kelley, American writer and TV producer (L.A. Law; Picket Fences; Chicago Hope; Boston Legal), born in Waterville, Maine
England cricket batsmanAndy Sandhamends Day 2 of 4th Test against West Indies in Kingston, Jamaica unbeaten on 309; 1st triple century in Test history; out for 325
British sitcom "The Good Life" starring Richard Briers, Felicity Kendal, Paul Eddington and Penelope Keith debuts on BBC One
Project Ozma begins at Green Bank radio astronomy center
Senegal declares independence from France
Second Defense Meteorological Satellite Program weather satellite is launched aboard a Titan II rocket
Battle ofRacławice, part of the Polish-Lithuanian uprising led byTadeusz Kościuszkowith a tactical Polish victory
Henry Cisneros becomes the first Mexican-American to serve as mayor of a major U.S. city, San Antonio
Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's last NBA game in Seattle
Congress decides on the design of the US flag: 13 red and white stripes and 20 stars
Golden Gate Park forms by City Order #800
Stanley Cup Final, Denman Arena, Vancouver, BC: Ottawa Senators (NHL) beat Vancouver Millionaires (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-2 series victory
Les Ames makes the 1st Test Cricket century by a wicketkeeper (149)
First march against nuclear weapons is held from London to Aldermaston in England, home of the Ministry of Defence's Atomic Weapons Establishment
First US Congress begins regular sessions duringGeorge Washington's presidency at Federal Hall, NYC (ends 1791)
Ashmun Institute, in Oxford, Pennsylvania, is renamed Lincoln University
Hugh Chalmers, automaker, suggests idea of baseball MVP
Warner Bros. Pictures, Inc. is formally incorporated by siblings Jack L. Warner, Harry Warner, Sam Warner and Albert Warner, in Burbank, California
William Cumming Rose, American biochemist who discovered threonine (an amino acid), born in Greenville, South Carolina (d. 1985)
Benjamin Pierce, American mathematician and astronomer (Peirce's criterion for outliers), born in Salem, Massachusetts (d. 1880)
Tad Lincoln, youngest son of U.S. president Abraham Lincoln, born in Springfield, Illinois (d. 1871)
Mary Colter, American architect and designer (Fred Harvey Company, Santa Fe Railroad), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1958)
